Bronze Breastplate  
 


Slot: CHEST
AC: 17
WT: 12.5 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BRD
Race: ALL except DWF TRL OGR HFL GNM IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)
Bronze Breastplate

Slot: CHEST
AC: 17
WT: 12.5 Size: LARGE
Class: WAR CLR PAL SHD BRD
Race: ALL except DWF TRL OGR HFL GNM IKS
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Slot 2, Type 21 (Special Ornamentation)

Item Type:Armor
Appearance:Plate
Tint:
 
Color (RGB):201, 102, 7
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:4 pp 4 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:18
Submitted By:Piasa

Item Updated By:Gidono
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-09 03:39:07
Page Updated:Thu Apr 20th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 20

a drolvarg gnasher
a drolvarg rager

a drolvarg snarler

greater kobold - Nagafen's Lair

Solusek kobold shaman

an elite goblin guard

Uploaded December 2nd, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Bronze is generally not worth it
QuoteReply
# Feb 13 2021 at 5:13 AM Rating: Decent
animekenji
1,123 posts
Unless you're a Dwarf or Halfling with maxed out strength and wearing small bronze, it's hard to justify the weight of this armor. Yeah, it's great protection, but if you don't have the strength to carry it, you won't be able to move or you'll only move very slowly. Even Ogres have a hard time with it because large bronze is a lot heavier than small or medium bronze. Just say no to bronze.
____________________________
If you were me and I were you, would we still be friends?
▲ Page top
Vah Shir Cannot Wear This
QuoteReply
# Jul 15 2018 at 12:33 PM Rating: Good
Whiffleball
Scholar
44 posts
All the standard Bronze Armor show a VAH can wear these items, but they cannot. It has to be Large. This is an error by Sony.
▲ Page top
Vah Shir Cannot Wear This
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2018 at 10:15 AM Rating: Excellent
Gidono
View User Forum
Moderator
6,835 posts
Whiffleball wrote:
All the standard Bronze Armor show a VAH can wear these items, but they cannot. It has to be Large. This is an error by Sony.


Have you tried equipping a VAH with regular bronze? I don't see any race/class restrictions on this armor for VAH.

QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 11:20 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592247182577
Scholar
110 posts
The two dwarves in WC only changes the size of Crafted Armor I believe.
▲ Page top
AC/wt
QuoteReply
# Aug 13 2001 at 10:12 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591765490650
1 post

Ok, some simple math

17AC 9.4 wt 9.4/17= .55wt per 1AC
17AC 12.5 wt 12.5/17= .74wt per 1AC

reverse for AC per wt

17AC 9.4 wt 17/9.4 = 1.81AC per 1wt
17AC 12.5 wt 17/12.5= 1.36AC per 1wt

I'm not flaming, just trying to set it straight

5lbs per 2AC would make this BP 47.5wt for 17AC, a bit ridiculous =)
▲ Page top
Weight
QuoteReply
# Jul 09 2001 at 2:33 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592344474330
Sage
3,705 posts
1.9 lbs for 2 ac seems like a lot of weight to take on for a small ac increase over banded. At least for my cleric. Maybe not for a warrior, I guess. Actually, its probably more for human sized, since the stats are for small bronze.
▲ Page top
RE: Weight
QuoteReply
# Jul 24 2001 at 8:36 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249352913
60 posts
The Stats for medium sized Bronze Breastplate:
AC 17
Weight: 12,5

That would be ~ 1,5 lbs per 2 AC

Small Bronze drops all over the place in Unrest, kill Ghouls and Crazed Ghouls, level is around mid to high 20s. Lots of stuff drops, never seen gaunts, boots or helm though

I am trying to get a full suit of small bronze armor, all i have left is the arm, leg, and boot component... and i am nearing 20...

so my question is what creature in those zones should i look for this to drop off of? specifically those three types mentioned above. Thanks to all who can help.

RE: What off of...
QuoteReply
# Jun 04 2001 at 2:41 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591759487642
View User Forum
6 posts
You can find the Bronze Leggings at Unrest. I played all day yesterday and retrieved 3 sets. You just have to be patient. Bronze collecting appears to be so random that you never know how much you are going of one thing you will end up with. I suggest trading with others inside the zone to complete your set.

Aldeberan
Cleric

# Apr 12 2001 at 6:54 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592756999176
61 posts
I do belive it is the ogres in this zone. YOu are too low I think to take the ogre camp here. he shaman and the chef con blue to me at 36. Plus the camp is next to the spawn point of two named cyclops. (I can handle one of the cycs with half mana, and don't fear them. But the chief on the other hand.... Lets just say I avoid the camp area.)
